steps.title: Identify the grammatical structures
steps.content: ”Interestingly, “ is an introductory adverb. The blank needs a complete clause (subject and verb), and “It first appears in the poem...” is another independent clause that follows.

steps.title: Apply the sentence type and punctuation rule
steps.content: Statements end with periods; questions end with question marks. Choices C and D begin with question word order (“was it,” “wasn't it”) but end with periods instead of question marks—breaking the rule that questions must end with question marks. Choice A begins with statement word order but ends with a question mark, an unusual mismatch. Choice B correctly pairs statement word order with a period. That matches choice B — B is correct.
